reasoning: The FrontierMath benchmark was designed to be exceptionally difficult, requiring hours or days for expert human mathematicians to solve. When launched in late 2024, top models scored around 2%. Over the course of 2025, performance improved due to test-time compute scaling (e.g., o3 reached 25% in late 2024), but began to plateau. As of October 2025, Epoch AI's evaluations showed that the absolute state-of-the-art model (GPT-5 high compute) only achieved 27-29% on the easier Tiers 1-3, and significantly less on the research-level Tier 4 (where models hover between 0% and 13%). Given the steep difficulty curve of these complex symbolic problems and a late 2025 maximum score of barely 30%, achieving over 80% within the remaining two months of 2025 is virtually impossible. Epoch AI itself stated in October 2025 that less than 70% "is within reach for today's models," and internal projections target 2027 for reaching an 80% score. Therefore, I assign the minimum possible probability.
